def:1.2.summary: A perfectoid field is a complete topological field $K$ whose topology is induced by a nondiscrete rank-$1$ valuation, such that the Frobenius $\Phi$ is surjective on $K^\circ/p$.

def:1.6.summary: A perfectoid $K$-algebra is a Banach $K$-algebra $R$ such that the powerbounded subring $R^\circ \subset R$ is bounded and Frobenius $\Phi$ is surjective on $R^\circ/p$.

thm:1.1.summary: The absolute Galois groups of $\mathbb{Q}_p(p^{1/p^\infty})$ and $\mathbb{F}_p((t))$ are canonically isomorphic. Concrete corollary of the general tilting equivalence; the headline reformulation of Fontaine–Wintenberger.

thm:1.3.summary: The absolute Galois groups of any perfectoid field $K$ and its tilt $K^\flat$ are canonically isomorphic. General version of thm:1.1.

thm:1.5.summary: There is a homeomorphism of topological spaces $|(\mathbb{A}^1_{K^\flat})^{\mathrm{ad}}| \cong \varprojlim_{T \mapsto T^p} |(\mathbb{A}^1_K)^{\mathrm{ad}}|$. The intuition pump for the whole tilting story.

thm:1.7.summary: Tilting equivalence: there is a natural equivalence of categories between perfectoid $K$-algebras and perfectoid $K^\flat$-algebras, with $R \mapsto R^\flat = \varprojlim_{x \mapsto x^p} R$. The central theorem of the paper.

thm:1.8.summary: For a perfectoid affinoid $K$-algebra $(R,R^+)$, the adic spectrum $X = \mathrm{Spa}(R,R^+)$ and its tilt $X^\flat$ are homeomorphic, and the structure presheaves are sheaves with vanishing higher cohomology in the almost category.

thm:1.9.summary: The categories of perfectoid spaces over $K$ and over $K^\flat$ are equivalent. Globalizes thm:1.7 and thm:1.8.

thm:1.10.summary: Almost purity (algebraic form): if $R$ is perfectoid and $S/R$ is finite étale, then $S$ is perfectoid and $S^\circ$ is almost finite étale over $R^\circ$. Recovers and extends Faltings's almost purity theorem.

thm:1.11.summary: For a perfectoid space $X$ over $K$ with tilt $X^\flat$, tilting induces an equivalence of étale sites $X_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}} \cong X^\flat_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}$.

thm:1.12.summary: The étale topos of $\mathbb{P}^{n,\mathrm{ad}}_{K^\flat}$ is equivalent to the inverse limit $\varprojlim_\varphi (\mathbb{P}^{n,\mathrm{ad}}_K)^\sim_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}$. Bridge to characteristic-$p$ side for weight-monodromy.

thm:1.14.summary: Weight-monodromy holds for any geometrically connected proper smooth variety over a local field of characteristic $0$ that is a set-theoretic complete intersection in a projective smooth toric variety. The arithmetic application that motivates the paper.

thm:3.7.summary: Field-level tilting equivalence: finite extensions of a perfectoid field $K$ correspond to finite extensions of its tilt $K^\flat$. Combined with Galois equivariance, this proves thm:1.3.

thm:5.2.summary: Algebra-level tilting equivalence factors as $K\text{-Perf} \cong K^{\circ a}\text{-Perf} \cong (K^{\circ a}/\varpi)\text{-Perf} = (K^{\flat\circ a}/\varpi^\flat)\text{-Perf} \cong K^{\flat\circ a}\text{-Perf} \cong K^\flat\text{-Perf}$. The proof of thm:1.7 in stages, using almost mathematics.

thm:5.10.summary: The functor $A \mapsto \overline{A} = A/\varpi$ induces an equivalence of categories $K^{\circ a}\text{-Perf} \cong (K^{\circ a}/\varpi)\text{-Perf}$. The deformation-theoretic engine of tilting — almost mathematics + cotangent-complex vanishing make modulo-$\varpi$ data lift uniquely.

prop:5.13.summary: Cotangent complex vanishing: $\mathbb{L}_{R/\mathbb{F}_p} \cong 0$ for perfect $\mathbb{F}_p$-algebras $R$, and a relative Frobenius criterion. The technical lemma making thm:5.10 work.

prop:5.5.summary: For $R$ perfectoid, $\Phi$ induces $R^\circ/\varpi^{1/p} \cong R^\circ/\varpi$, and $A = R^{\circ a}$ is a perfectoid $K^{\circ a}$-algebra. Connects definition (perfectoid) to almost-mathematics objects.

prop:5.8.summary: Every perfectoid $K$-algebra $R$ is reduced. Used pervasively when handling powerbounded elements.

prop:5.9.summary: In characteristic $p$, a Banach $K$-algebra with bounded powerbounded subring is perfectoid iff it is perfect. Lets the characteristic-$p$ side of tilting be checked via perfectness.

prop:5.22.summary: If $\overline{A}$ is a perfectoid $K^{\circ a}/\varpi$-algebra and $\overline{B}/\overline{A}$ is finite étale, then $\overline{B}$ is perfectoid. The mod-$\varpi$ stability of perfectoid under finite étale.

prop:5.23.summary: Almost purity in characteristic $p$: for $R$ perfectoid over a perfectoid field of characteristic $p$ and $S/R$ finite étale, $S$ is perfectoid and $S^{\circ a}$ is finite étale (and uniformly finitely projective) over $R^{\circ a}$. The char-$p$ base case of thm:1.10.

thm:5.25.summary: Fully faithful functor $R^\flat_{\mathrm{f\acute{e}t}} \to R_{\mathrm{f\acute{e}t}}$ inverse to tilting; essential image consists of finite étale covers $S$ with $S$ perfectoid and $S^{\circ a}$ finite étale over $R^{\circ a}$. Combining with prop:5.23 lifts almost purity to characteristic $0$, proving thm:1.10.

lem:6.2.summary: Perfectoid affinoid $K$-algebras and perfectoid affinoid $K^\flat$-algebras are equivalent categories; tilting is given by $R^{\flat+} = \varprojlim_{x \mapsto x^p} R^+$ and induces $R^{\flat+}/\varpi^\flat \cong R^+/\varpi$. Affinoid version of thm:1.7.

thm:6.3.summary: For a perfectoid affinoid $K$-algebra $(R,R^+)$, $X = \mathrm{Spa}(R,R^+)$ has $X \cong X^\flat$ as topological spaces, $\mathcal{O}_X$ is a sheaf, and the almost structure sheaf has vanishing higher cohomology. Proves thm:1.8.

prop:6.14.summary: Almost-Tate acyclicity: for perfectoid affinoids and any finite rational cover, the Čech complex of $\mathcal{O}_X^{\circ a}$ is exact. The vanishing input to thm:6.3.

prop:6.17.summary: Every perfectoid space $X$ over $K$ has a tilt $X^\flat$, unique up to unique isomorphism, giving the equivalence between perfectoid spaces over $K$ and over $K^\flat$. Proves thm:1.9 by globalizing lem:6.2 and thm:6.3.

prop:7.4.summary: Henselian rigidity: for $A$ flat over $K^\circ$ and henselian along $(\varpi)$, finite étale $A[\varpi^{-1}]$-algebras correspond to finite étale $\hat{A}[\varpi^{-1}]$-algebras. The henselian-completion bridge for almost purity in characteristic $0$.

prop:7.7.summary: In characteristic $p$, an étale map of perfectoid spaces is locally pulled back from an étale map of noetherian adic spaces. Reduces étale geometry to classical adic spaces in char $p$.

thm:7.9.summary: For $(R,R^+)$ perfectoid affinoid: strongly finite étale covers of open affinoids $V \subset X$ are equivalent to finite étale covers of $\mathcal{O}_X(V)$. The site-level statement underlying thm:1.11.

thm:7.12.summary: Étale-site tilting: for any perfectoid space $X$ over $K$, tilting gives an isomorphism of sites $X_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}} \cong X^\flat_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}$, functorial in $X$. Proves thm:1.11.

thm:7.17.summary: $X_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}^\sim$ is the projective limit of the fibred topos $(X_{i,\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}^\sim)_i$ for a tower of noetherian adic spaces with perfectoid limit. Lets one transport étale information across the perfectoid limit.

thm:9.4.summary: Weight-monodromy holds in the equal-characteristic case: for a curve $C/\mathbb{F}_q$, $x \in C(\mathbb{F}_q)$, and $X$ proper smooth over $C \setminus \{x\}$, weight-monodromy holds for $X_k$. Deligne's char-$p$ result, used as the base case via tilting.

thm:9.6.summary: Weight-monodromy in characteristic $0$ for set-theoretic complete intersections in projective smooth toric varieties. Proved by tilting the variety inside $\mathbb{P}^{n,\mathrm{ad}}_K$, using thm:1.12 and prop:8.X (toric perfectoid covers) to reduce to thm:9.4.

edges.note: Apply general perfectoid Galois equivalence to $K = \mathbb{Q}_p(p^{1/p^\infty})$, with tilt $K^\flat = \mathbb{F}_p((t))$.
Galois equivalence is the automorphism-group form of the finite-extension equivalence.
Concrete instance of $|X| \cong |X^\flat|$ for the perfectoid affine line.
Body proof of the tilting equivalence.
Body proof of affinoid-level tilting + sheaf property.
Body proof of perfectoid-space tilting.
Body proof of almost purity, lifted from char $p$ via tilting.
Body proof of étale-site tilting.
Inverse-limit description of $\mathbb{P}^n$ étale topos uses tilting on the projective adic perfectoid cover.
Topos limit comparison in the tower of $p$-power Frobenius pullbacks.
Body version of the weight-monodromy application.
Field tilting is internal to the perfectoid-field category.
Mod-$\varpi$ equivalence is the central step in the chain of equivalences.
Connects the analytic perfectoid algebra to its almost ring of integers.
Char-$p$ side of the chain reduces to perfect Banach algebras.
Equivalence is over the category defined here.
Cotangent-complex vanishing supplies the obstruction-theoretic input that lifts mod-$\varpi$ data uniquely.
Frobenius surjectivity mod $\varpi^{1/p}$ is the structural property exploited.
Char-$p$ almost purity is the base case; tilting transports it to char $0$.
Tilting equivalence transports the finite-étale category.
Henselian-completion bridge identifies finite étale covers across completion.
Mod-$\varpi$ étale extensions are perfectoid; lift to characteristic-$p$ algebras via thm:5.10.
Lifts mod-$\varpi$ finite-étale data to integral level uniquely.
Mod-$\varpi$ equivalence is internal to perfectoid algebras.
Affinoid version reuses the algebra-level tilting.
Topological identification $|X| \cong |X^\flat|$ comes from the affinoid tilt.
Almost-Tate acyclicity is the sheaf and cohomology-vanishing input.
Globalize the affinoid statement to perfectoid spaces.
Glue affinoid tilts compatibly.
Reduce étale geometry over perfectoid affinoids to noetherian adic-space étale geometry in char $p$.
Transfers finite étale algebras across $\varpi$-adic completion.
Étale-site equivalence is the global form of the finite-étale equivalence on affinoids.
Almost purity guarantees finite-étale covers stay inside the perfectoid world, making the site equivalence well-defined.
Char-$0$ weight-monodromy reduces to the classical char-$p$ result via tilting.
$\mathbb{P}^n$ étale-topos limit description is the geometric bridge between char $0$ and char $p$.
Étale-site tilting applied to a small neighborhood of $Y$ inside $\mathbb{P}^{n,\mathrm{ad}}_K$.
Reducedness of perfectoid algebras simplifies the powerbounded subring analysis.

foundations.description: Definitions of perfectoid field and perfectoid algebra. Set up the categories tilting equates.

field_level_tilting.description: Section 3. Establish the Fontaine–Wintenberger-style equivalence between finite extensions of $K$ and $K^\flat$, hence Galois group isomorphism.

algebra_level_tilting.description: Section 5. The tilting equivalence at the level of perfectoid algebras, factored through the almost category and the mod-$\varpi$ reduction. The deformation engine $\thm{5.10}$ powered by cotangent-complex vanishing.

almost_purity.description: Section 5 / 7. Almost purity: finite étale covers of perfectoid algebras stay perfectoid and become finite étale in the almost category. Char-$p$ base case lifted to char $0$ via tilting and henselian-completion bridges.

spaces_and_analytic_topology.description: Section 6. Globalize the algebra statements to adic spaces: the topological space, the structure presheaf as a sheaf, the almost-Tate acyclicity, and tilting on perfectoid spaces.

etale_topology.description: Section 7. Strongly étale = finite étale on affinoids; tilting equates étale sites; the $\mathbb{P}^n$ étale topos as an inverse limit. The geometric machinery the weight-monodromy argument runs on.

weight_monodromy_application.description: Sections 8–9. Tilting a complete-intersection variety in a projective smooth toric variety to characteristic $p$, where Deligne's curve-base case (thm:9.4) gives the result. The arithmetic payoff that motivates the framework.

FontaineWintenberger.title: Le 'corps des normes' de certaines extensions algébriques de corps locaux
FontaineWintenberger.role: Classical predecessor of tilting (char $0$ ↔ char $p$ Galois groups).

FaltingsAlmostEtale.role: Almost mathematics; precursor of almost purity.

GabberRamero.title: Almost ring theory
GabberRamero.role: Systematic foundations for almost mathematics; deformation theory used in thm:5.10–5.12.

HuberContVal.title: Continuous valuations

HuberDefAdic.title: A generalization of formal schemes and rigid analytic varieties

DeligneWeil2.title: La conjecture de Weil II

IllusieCotangent.title: Complexe cotangent et déformations I
IllusieCotangent.role: Cotangent complex; obstruction theory used in thm:5.11–5.12.

KedlayaLiu.title: Relative p-adic Hodge theory: Foundations
KedlayaLiu.role: Parallel development of relative perfectoid-style theory.

Caraiani.title: Local-global compatibility and the action of monodromy on nearby cycles

HarrisTaylor.title: The geometry and cohomology of some simple Shimura varieties

BerkovichSpectralTheory.title: Spectral theory and analytic geometry over non-Archimedean fields

TateRigidAnalytic.title: Rigid analytic spaces
TateRigidAnalytic.role: Original rigid-analytic spaces, predecessor to adic spaces.

deJongAlterations.title: Smoothness, semi-stability and alterations
deJongAlterations.role: Used as an input to weight-monodromy reductions.

Boyer.title: Monodromie du faisceau pervers des cycles évanescents...

RapoportZink.role: Rapoport–Zink moduli spaces; later perfectoid-ized in 1306.6427.

Shin.title: Galois representations arising from some compact Shimura varieties

ItoUpperHalfPlane.role: Partial weight-monodromy via $p$-adic upper half-plane uniformization.

Terasoma.title: Monodromy weight filtration is independent of $\ell$

Dat.title: Espaces symétriques de Drinfeld and Lefschetz formula

Boyer2.role: Local monodromy on Shimura varieties, follow-up.
